response.justification: Wei Chen is a strong individual contributor with 15+ years of software development experience and genuine developer-tooling interests (test runners, assertion libraries, CI setup, MCP code generators), but the role is a Level II people-management position requiring 2+ years of people management and 3+ years of formal technical leadership — neither of which appears on the resume. There is also no mobile-platform ecosystem depth at the scale implied (one iOS/Apple TV stint in 2015-2016), and no evidence of leading organizational change or managing team performance. The gap is in the core of the role, not the periphery.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency systems
Authentic developer-tools track record: test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD infrastructure, Selenium training, MCP generators
Strong quality culture: TDD, trunk-based development, code katas, workshops, and community teaching
concerns: No formal people-management experience (one-on-ones, performance management, team development)
No demonstrated strategic roadmap ownership or organizational-change leadership
Mobile-platform experience is dated and brief relative to the role's mobile ecosystem focus

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure background
